content.text:
Now I have a clear view of the molecule. Looking at atom C:15 (carbon with index 15), I can clearly see its covalent connections:

1. C:14 (carbon) - connected to the left
2. C:16 (carbon) - connected to the upper left
3. H:19 (hydrogen) - connected to the upper right

From the image, atom C:15 has exactly three covalent bonds: two to carbon atoms (C:14 and C:16) and one to a hydrogen atom (H:19). This makes sense for a carbon atom in an organic molecule, likely part of a ring structure given the connectivity.

The geomconf_index for this molecule is 4863, as indicated by the filename "geomconf_4863.xyz".

So the neighbor atoms of atom index 15 (C:15) are:
- Atom index 14, element C
- Atom index 16, element C  
- Atom index 19, element H

And the geomconf_index is 4863.

question: Please find the neighbour atoms of the atom with atomic index 15, whose element should be C. Here, neighbour atoms are defined as atoms that are covalently connected to this atom. Report all neighbour atoms by listing their atomic index (as shown in the image) and their element. Also return the geomconf_index for this molecule.
